description_long: This object consists of a cluster of three large, rounded mushrooms with deep reddish-brown, glossy caps that have a smooth, slightly domed surface and subtle water droplets scattered across them. The caps are thick and robust, with a uniform coloration and a moist, almost lacquered appearance. Each mushroom is supported by a short, sturdy stem that is light brown and has a slightly fibrous texture, resembling natural wood. The stems are relatively thick in proportion to the caps, giving the mushrooms a squat, stable look. The underside of the caps is not visible, so the presence of gills or pores cannot be confirmed. The overall form is organic and naturalistic, suggesting these mushrooms could be found in a park or landscaped urban green space.
description: A cluster of three large, glossy, reddish-brown mushrooms with smooth domed caps and thick, light brown stems. The caps are moist and rounded, with water droplets visible on the surface.
description_view_0: Three mushrooms are visible, with the largest cap in the foreground and two others partially overlapping behind, showing the smooth, shiny surface and water droplets.
description_view_1: The cluster is seen from a different angle, highlighting the rounded caps and the thick stems, with all three mushrooms clearly visible.
description_view_2: Only two mushrooms are visible, with the caps dominating the view and the stems partially obscured, emphasizing the size and glossiness of the caps.
description_view_3: The largest mushroom is prominent, with a second, smaller mushroom to the side, both showing the smooth, shiny cap surfaces and thick stems.
